steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the Spanish architectural influence across California. The key evidence: visitors to buildings like Pasadena City Hall "can easily recognize" how the style draws from Spanish missions. The word must mean clearly and unmistakably felt or perceptible.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — palpable: Means so strong it can almost be physically felt — clearly and powerfully perceptible. That meaning aligns exactly with tourists easily recognizing the Spanish mission influence in buildings across the state. ✓
  • Choice B — understated: Means subtle or deliberately downplayed. The passage says people can easily recognize the influence, which is the opposite of understated. ✗
  • Choice C — corroborated: Means confirmed or backed up by separate evidence. An influence cannot corroborate itself; the word says nothing about how visible or widespread the influence is. ✗
  • Choice D — disputed: Means contested or debated. The passage treats the Spanish influence as a straightforward, observable fact — there is no controversy. ✗
That is the correct answer.
